title: Revision Patch Document
description: #89 Item 7 patch document (spec docs/design/2026-06-10-390-diff-patch-revision-mode-spec.md §3.2). The writer's revision deliverable: a closed-vocabulary list of block operations against an anchored base draft. Every hash in this document — base_draft_hash and every per-op old_hash — is a mechanical copy from the script-generated block manifest (§3.1); the writer never computes hashes. The DOC-BODY-START insert_after shape is the ONLY legal hash-less op: it names a position (after frontmatter, before the first body block), not an anchor block, so there is no content to precondition on. Schema validation is the apply script's step zero; everything the schema cannot see (target existence, hash staleness, per-block single-op rule, marker-free new_text) is Phase 1's job (§3.3).

patch_format_version.description: Pinned so a future Item 9 revision contract can audit exactly what shape it gates (spec §7).

revision_round.description: 1-based revision round this patch belongs to. Each patch is generated per-round against the current base (§3.1 trigger point c).

base_draft_hash.description: First 12 hex chars of SHA-256 over the anchored base file's raw bytes — copied from the block manifest's base_draft_hash, never computed by the writer. Document-level staleness gate: any mismatch rejects the whole patch (§3.3 Phase 1).

ops.description: Block operations, defined simultaneously against the base — array order is not semantic; the apply script splices in base-document order (§3.2). Each block_id may appear in at most one op, in any role (Phase 1 enforces; the schema cannot see across items).

emitted_by.description: Emitting agent (e.g. draft_writer_agent). Provenance surface only; the role split itself is enforced by the #134 write guard, not by this field (§3.5).

roadmap_item_ids.description: Schema 7 RoadmapItem IDs this op addresses. Presence is machine-checked; semantic fit is an LLM/human-layer judgment (§3.2 — Anti-Pattern 7 made visible, not impossible).

new_text.description: Replacement / inserted markdown. MUST NOT contain <!--block: markers (ID assignment is the apply script's exclusive authority) and MUST carry v3.7.1/v3.7.3 citation markers for any citation it introduces (§3.2). Segmented by the shared parser; multi-block text receives fresh IDs at apply time.
